admission_type: open access with entry requirements examination
academic_requirements: Admission requires a Bachelor's degree (or three-year university diploma) in one of the recognised degree classes including L-2 Biotechnologies, L-7 Civil and Environmental Engineering, L-8 Information Technology Engineering, L-9 Industrial Engineering, L-13 Biology, L-23 Building Sciences, L-25 Agriculture and Forestry, L-26 Food Sciences, L-27 Chemistry, L-28 Navigation, L-29 Pharmacy, L-30 Physics, L-31 Computer Science, L-32 Environmental Sciences, L-34 Earth Sciences, L-35 Mathematics, L-38 Zootechnical sciences, L-41 Statistics, L-43 Diagnostics for the conservation of cultural heritage, L-Sc.Mat Materials Science; or equivalent qualifications including LM-6/7/8/9/10/11/18/20/21/27/29/32/33/34/40/58/60/71/75/79. Candidates with degrees in L-27 Chemistry or L-30 Physics are exempt from interview. Assessment by Evaluation Committee verifies basic knowledge of Chemistry, Materials Physics, laboratory instrumentation, experimental data analysis, and mathematics for modelling.
language_requirements: B2 level of English (CEFR) is required. Candidates must upload one of: 1) An accepted B2-or-higher certificate not older than 3 years (UNIMIB's list of accepted certifications includes IELTS, TOEFL iBT, Cambridge English, TOEIC, Trinity ISE, etc.); 2) UNIMIB's Bbetween English B2 Open Badge; 3) Self-certification of at least 4 CFU exam in L-LIN/10, L-LIN/11 or L-LIN/12; 4) Passport from an English-speaking country (Antigua and Barbuda, Australia, Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Canada, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Ireland, Jamaica, Malta, New Zealand, St. Kitts and Nevis, St. Lucia, St. Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, United Kingdom, USA). Alternatively candidates can take the UNIMIB English test (in person).
entry_exam_or_test: Admission interview by the Evaluation Committee, focused on Chemistry, Materials Physics, lab instrumentation/techniques and mathematics. Candidates exempt from interview: holders of an Italian Bachelor's or Master's degree in L-27 Chemistry, L-30 Physics, L-Sc.Mat Materials Science, or equivalent. A CV evaluation also forms part of the process. Application fee: EUR 30.
application_deadline_eu: 2026/2027 A.Y. — Italian-qualification candidates apply via Segreterie OnLine within the programme's call windows (refer to the official MSN 2026/27 call PDF when published). Foreign-qualification EU candidates (and non-EU residing in Italy) apply via apply.unimib.it (Dream Apply) in the unified UNIMIB Master 2026/27 calls: 1st CALL 10 February - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL 15 April - 6 July 2026; 3rd CALL 7 July - 7 September 2026 (3rd CALL: EU + non-EU residing in Italy only).
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU candidates requiring a visa apply via apply.unimib.it (Dream Apply) in the earlier 2026/27 Master calls only: 1st CALL 10 February - 31 March 2026; 2nd CALL 15 April - 6 July 2026. The 3rd CALL (7 July - 7 September 2026) is reserved for EU + non-EU residing in Italy. English certification by 31 July 2026; arrival in Italy by 31 January 2027; original-document verification by 28 February 2027.
required_documents: 1) Valid ID document (identity card or passport); 2) Digital ID photo in bitmap or JPEG, minimum 300x400 pixels; 3) MANDATORY self-certification (Dichiarazione Sostitutiva di Certificazione) attesting academic qualification, graduation mark, passed exams, obtained CFUs and Settori Scientifico-Disciplinari (SSD), signed by the candidate; for graduating students, self-certification of enrolment plus passed exams; 4) Proof of B2 English language knowledge; 5) Optional: other titles/statements of relevant activities; 6) Payment of EUR 30 application fee. Foreign-qualification candidates additionally upload official transcripts, degree certificate/Diploma Supplement, CV, motivation letter and passport copy on Dream Apply.

source_quotes.quote: Degree programme language: English. Access: Open access with entry requirements examination.

Admission to the Master's degree programme in Materials Science and Nanotechnology (LM Sc.Mat.) is subject to the possession of specific curricular requirements and to the adequacy of personal preparation verified by an Evaluation Committee.
Have an English language level of B2 or higher to enable understanding and participation in the training activities.

Candidates who have obtained, or are in the process of obtaining, a Bachelor's degree or Master's degree belonging to one of the following classes... L-27 Chemistry, L-30 Physics... will be exempt from the interview.

Application deadlines: From 1 May to 15 June 2025 (interview 27 June 2025); From 16 June to 10 September 2025 (interview 18 September 2025); From 8 January to 26 January 2026 (interview 11 February 2026).
Applicants with a foreign academic qualification must apply exclusively via Dream Apply.

uncertainty_notes: The official Materials Science 2026/2027 call PDF was not yet published at research time; deadlines for the Italian-qualification path are from the 2025/26 call PDF. Foreign-qualification 2026/27 deadlines are from UNIMIB's apply.unimib.it general Master's calendar. UNIMIB does not specify a minimum CFU count in CHIM/FIS/MAT subject areas; eligibility is determined by Bachelor's degree class. Minimum IELTS/TOEFL numerical scores are defined in UNIMIB's 'Certificazioni_INGLESE_nov2025_UNIMIB.pdf' (B2 equivalent, typically IELTS 5.5+/TOEFL iBT 80+). Deadlines re-verified 2026-06-11 against apply.unimib.it 2026/27 calendar (Master track: 1st CALL 10 Feb - 31 Mar 2026; 2nd CALL 15 Apr - 6 Jul 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]; 3rd CALL 7 Jul - 7 Sep 2026 [EU + non-EU in Italy]). The previously inserted 4-call Nov 2025 - Sep 2026 schedule is the Bachelor/single-cycle calendar and was incorrect for this Master.
